definitions: In this section an overview will be given of the used terms and definition specifically of this product.
service_summary:
This section gives a brief summary of the described Service.
The Service covers all tasks and activities required to deliver the Backup & Recovery Service (also referred to as 'Backup-as-a-Service'). Through this managed service offering, Cegeka aims to relieve the Customer of the technical responsibilities associated with managing and operating a backup platform.
The standard Service features are designed to ensure the platform supporting the Backup & Recovery Service remains operational, secure, and technically optimized.
services:
Cegeka’s Backup & Recovery Service provides comprehensive protection and recovery capabilities for systems and data hosted in Cegeka datacenters, as well as on satellite locations such as public cloud environments and customer-operated datacenters or datarooms.
The service is built on the Commvault backup and recovery software platform and operates on Cegeka-owned, secured, and isolated infrastructure. It includes the full management of backup processes and the secure storage of backup data. The service functions independently from all other Cegeka services to ensure the highest level of availability.
standard services:
This section describes the activities which are executed as part of the the Service.
Technical Management
Cegeka is responsible for the technical management required to deliver the Backup & Recovery Service and for ensuring its proper functioning during the agreed availability windows.
Technical management of the Backup & Recovery Service includes the following core (platform) activities:
Objective: ensuring the backup platform is available, maintained, and functioning correctly
Objective: ensuring data is protected according to defined customer requirements
Objective: ensuring customers can recover data when needed
Objective: managing long-term retention and secure offline copies
Objective: ensuring backups meet regulatory, retention, and security expectations
Security and Compliance
Security and compliance are central to any Service delivered by Cegeka. Cegeka ensures that all infrastructure components are deployed, configured, and maintained in line with recognized security standards and agreed contractual requirements. The goal is to protect the infrastructure component and its data while aligning with both Cegeka’s internal security practices and the Customer’s specific policies.
For this particular Service, the supporting platform -based on Commvault technology- has been configured follwing the vendor's security guidelines, which outlines and support Center for Internet Security (CIS) Level 1 benchmarks for hardening.
Security incident management is performed in accordance with Cegeka’s internal policies as well as the contractual security requirements of the Customer. When a potential breach is detected, a security incident ticket is created and communicated without delay to both the Customer and the relevant Cegeka stakeholders.
Patch Management
Effective patch management is critical for keeping systems up to date, minimizing the attack surface, and ensuring that the infrastructure, operating system, applications, etc... remain fully supported. This section outlines the patch policy governing the service and describes the specific approach applied to each component.
Regular patching is a fundamental element of the Service’s operational management. High-risk vulnerabilities requiring urgent remediation will be addressed outside the standard patching cycle through expedited updates.
Upgrades are performed only when recommended by the vendor, based on internal metrics such as run hours, version stability, and identified defects. The following sections describe the patching policy applicable to this service.
Monitoring
The infrastructure components (both hardware and software) that make up the Backup & Recovery Service platform, are by default monitored through Cegeka's Monitoring Platform. Monitoring involves tracking essential operating parameters and ensuring that the required services and processes remain available. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in line with the agreed Service Level for the specific environment. Significant events are classified as incidents and handled through the standard incident management process. Additionally, when the Threat Scanning feature is activated, monitoring is extended and integrated with Cegeka's Modern SOC.
optional services:
This section outlines the optional services which may be added to the list of standard services and -when applicable- will be priced in addition to the standard offering.
The following options can be enabled, yet require a technical review prior to activation:
When activated for customers who also use Cegeka’s Modern SOC services, Threat Scanning is seamlessly integrated to provide enhanced analytical context and to help ensure that any required response is executed as effectively as possible.
Activation of any optional technical features or service extensions requires the creation of an RFC, which will be handled in accordance with the standard change process. Additionally, the Backup Plan will be updated to reflect the changes introduced by these new features and services.
* Both service extensions, Tape to Disk Offloading and Air-gapped Tape Vault, are available only to customers who are already onboarded into the Backup & Recovery Service. Access to these extensions requires consumption of the core service and they cannot be offered as standalone solutions.
** Near-infinite retention for Microsoft 365 backup, guarantees a 10 year retention. In case of GDPR concerns, retention can be lowered to 1 month. A fair use policy applies with regards to storage consumption: Cegeka does not impose hard limits on storage utilization but reserves the right to charge additional costs if utilization is disproportionate against common industry average and/or averages across our install base.
requests:
The handling of requests -either standard or non-standard- is subject to the regular change management process (see Enabling Services). Standard Change Requests Standard change requests related to the Service, can be requested when available. A standard change request is a pre-approved and pre-defined service request, often used for recurring needs that arise multiple times per month. All standard change requests are processed as change tickets and are delivered within the indicated lead time. Wherever possible, the execution of these requests is optimally automated, ensuring efficiency and consistency. Standard change requests are offered at a fixed price. | Name | LEAD TIME (*) | | Modify an existing backup policy | 1 business day | | Create a new backup plan | 1 business day | | Modify the existing backup plan | 1 business day | | Remove host from backup (incl. data removal) | 1 business day | | Add an host to backup | 2-6 business days Depends on the execution speed of the pre-requisites (eg. agent installation, network configuration, etc…) | | Add an SQL instance to backup | 1 business day | | Disable the backup process of a specific host | 1 business day |
Name LEAD TIME (*) Modify an existing backup policy 1 business day Create a new backup plan 1 business day Modify the existing backup plan 1 business day Remove host from backup (incl. data removal) 1 business day Add an host to backup 2-6 business days Depends on the execution speed of the pre-requisites (eg. agent installation, network configuration, etc…) Add an SQL instance to backup 1 business day Disable the backup process of a specific host 1 business day * estimated lead time applies from the moment the change request has been received by the delivery team. Non-standard Change Requests Next to Standard Change Requests, the Service also facilitates and supports non-standard change requests when applicable. Non-standard change requests are requests which are not pre-approved nor pre-defined and therefore require a thorough evaluation and analysis. In such cases, Cegeka prepares a proposal along with an impact assessment and a time-and-materials (T&M) estimation to address the request. Before execution, the proposal must undergo review, approval, and planning by the Change Advisory Board (CAB), including the scheduling of an appropriate maintenance window. Non-standard change requests are billed on a T&M basis.
out_of_scope: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope.
obligatory: This section outlines the additional services that must be in place for Cegeka to effectively deliver the described Service.
conditions:
This section defines the conditions that must be fulfilled for Cegeka to deliver the described Service effectively.
Prerequisites :
Conditions :
cegeka owned licenses and/or subscriptio:
The Service and its corresponding pricing model, includes all required licenses to operate the platform in compliance with the vendors licensing model. This includes licenses required to enable specific features and/or functionality (eg. Threat Scanning)
It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Enabling Services are included in the Service and are not listed below.
When Cegeka is responsible for maintaining license and/or subscription compliance, the following related administrative activities are provided and included in the Service :
customer_licenses:
When applicable - Licenses and/or subscriptions owned by the Customer, as well as their associated costs, are not included in this Service. In this scenario, the Customer is responsible for ensuring compliance with all applicable licenses and subscriptions.
To support this, the Customer must provide Cegeka with the relevant license information. Cegeka will assist in maintaining compliance by sharing expertise, reporting on license usage and requirements.
N/A for this Service as all required platform licenses are owned by Cegeka (cfr. 2.3.3)
location:
This section mentions the location from which this service is delivered.
This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) office environment.
ownership:
This section defines the ownership of the equipment related to the service.
N/A for this Service as underlying infrastructure equipment is owned by Cegeka
misc:
In this section specific topics regarding the Service will be listed and shortly described.
Data Access and Confidentiality :
Cegeka does not require access to confidential data in order to deliver this Service. The credentials used by Cegeka engineers do not provide access to such data for the purpose of this Service. In the event of functional issue investigations, Cegeka personnel may incidentally view or receive fragments of such data if it is shared by the Customer (eg. exports, screensharing or screenshots).
Service Reporting :
Monthly reporting will be provided to confirm service availability, demonstrate adherence to SLAs, and deliver insights into current capacity usage and anticipated expansion requirements.
Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer:
This service is delivered with:

service_level:
Service Availability For the Backup & Recovery Service, two Service Availability Levels are defined. One covers the percentage of successful backups and one the percentage of successful restores, each with its corresponding Service Level Objective (SLO). Availability is measured at the level of an environment or a specific service, which may refer to an entire solution or an individual infrastructure component. Cegeka may define specific infrastructure requirements to ensure that a given environment can meet the targeted Service Level Objective (SLO). | AVAILABILITY PARAMETERS | | | SLO Measuring Window | Window in which the service is offered | | Backup % | | | Restore % | A restore is considered successful when the existing data has been replaced with the data contained in the backup. The service does not guarantee the restored data is correct or up to date. It only guarantees that the data from the backup is restored exactly as it was. |
AVAILABILITY PARAMETERS SLO Measuring Window Window in which the service is offered Backup % Restore % A restore is considered successful when the existing data has been replaced with the data contained in the backup. The service does not guarantee the restored data is correct or up to date. It only guarantees that the data from the backup is restored exactly as it was. The following Service Availability Levels are provided for the Backup & Recovery Service: | ID | NAME | SERVICE WINDOW | METRIC | | KPI.0.L.133.001 | Backup successful | Premium | % successful backups | | KPI.0.L.133.002 | Restore successful | Premium | % successful restores |
ID NAME SERVICE WINDOW METRIC KPI.0.L.133.001 Backup successful Premium % successful backups KPI.0.L.133.002 Restore successful Premium % successful restores
